        "policy": "Additional project context acquired from clean proof-lane decline notes after same-source target-declaration exclusion filtering. Use these snippets as visible project context; do not infer hidden aligned target declarations.",
        "selected_project_context": [
          {
            "context_source": "proof_lane_decline_context_miner_project_source_candidate",
            "identifier_from_decline_note": "NPartition.transpose",
            "kind": "def",
            "lean_snippet": "noncomputable def transpose (μ : NPartition N) (hN : 0 < N) : NPartition (μ.parts ⟨0, hN⟩) where\n  parts := fun i => (Finset.univ.filter fun j : Fin N => i.val + 1 ≤ μ.parts j).card\n  antitone := by\n    intro i j hij\n    apply Finset.card_le_card\n    intro r hr\n    simp only [Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_univ, true_and] at hr ⊢\n    exact le_trans (Nat.add_le_add_right hij 1) hr\n\n/-- Predicate asserting that `lamt` is the transpose of `lam`.\n    The transpose λᵗ of a partition λ satisfies:\n    (λᵗ)ᵢ = |{j : λⱼ ≥ i}| for each i.\n\n    Since we work with fixed-length tuples, this predicate captures\n    when two tuples represent transpose partitions (possibly with trailing zeros). -/",
            "line": 1057,
            "match_kind": "exact_full_name",
            "name": "NPartition.transpose",
            "path": "algebraic-combinatorics/AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/NPartition.lean",
            "safety_policy": "Candidate came from a target-hidden proof-lane decline note and project-source scan. Same-source aligned/referencing target declarations were used only as an exclusion filter and are not included in this pack."
          },
          {
            "context_source": "proof_lane_decline_context_miner_project_source_candidate",
            "identifier_from_decline_note": "NPartition.transpose",
            "kind": "def",
            "lean_snippet": "noncomputable def transpose (lam : NPartition N) (hN : 0 < N) : NPartition (lam.parts ⟨0, hN⟩) where\n  parts := fun i => (Finset.univ.filter fun j : Fin N => i.val + 1 ≤ lam.parts j).card\n  weaklyDecreasing := by\n    intro i j hij\n    apply Finset.card_le_card\n    intro r hr\n    simp only [Finset.mem_filter, Finset.mem_univ, true_and] at hr ⊢\n    exact le_trans (Nat.add_le_add_right hij 1) hr\n\n/-- Predicate asserting that `lamt` is the transpose of `lam`.\n    The transpose λᵗ of a partition λ satisfies:\n    (λᵗ)ᵢ = |{j : λⱼ ≥ i}| for each i.\n\n    Since we work with fixed-length tuples, this predicate captures\n    when two tuples represent transpose partitions (possibly with trailing zeros). -/",
            "line": 159,
            "match_kind": "suffix_full_name",
            "name": "SymmetricFunctions.NPartition.transpose",
            "path": "algebraic-combinatorics/AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.lean",
            "safety_policy": "Candidate came from a target-hidden proof-lane decline note and project-source scan. Same-source aligned/referencing target declarations were used only as an exclusion filter and are not included in this pack."
          },
          {
            "context_source": "proof_lane_decline_context_miner_project_source_candidate",
            "identifier_from_decline_note": "NPartition.transpose",
            "kind": "def",
            "lean_snippet": "noncomputable def transpose {n : ℕ} (p : Partition n) : Partition n := by\n  -- The transpose has parts: for each i from 1 to (largest part of p),\n  -- the i-th part of the transpose is the number of parts of p that are ≥ i\n  let largest := p.parts.fold max 0\n  let newParts : Multiset ℕ := (Finset.range largest).val.map\n    (fun i => (p.parts.filter (· > i)).card)\n  refine ⟨newParts.filter (· > 0), ?_, ?_⟩\n  · intro i hi\n    simp only [Multiset.mem_filter] at hi\n    exact hi.2\n  · -- Prove sum equals n using double counting\n    show (newParts.filter (· > 0)).sum = n\n    have h1 : newParts.sum = n := by\n      show ((Finset.range largest).val.map (fun i => (p.parts.filter (· > i)).card)).sum = n\n      have heq : ((Finset.range largest).val.map (fun i => (p.parts.filter (· > i)).card)).sum =\n                 (Finset.range largest).sum (fun i => (p.parts.filter (· > i)).card) := rfl\n      rw [heq]\n      have hp' : ∀ i ∈ p.parts, 0 < i := fun i hi => p.parts_pos hi\n      have := sum_filter_card_eq_sum p.parts hp'\n      rw [p.parts_sum] at this\n      exact this\n    rw [sum_filter_positive newParts]\n    exact h1\n\n/-- Helper lemma: for a sorted decreasing list, the count of elements > i is > j iff the j-th element > i.\n    This is the key bijection for the Young diagram transpose. -/",
            "line": 1721,
            "match_kind": "exact_local_name",
            "name": "Nat.Partition.transpose",
            "path": "algebraic-combinatorics/AlgebraicCombinatorics/Partitions/Basics.lean",
            "safety_policy": "Candidate came from a target-hidden proof-lane decline note and project-source scan. Same-source aligned/referencing target declarations were used only as an exclusion filter and are not included in this pack."
          },
          {
            "context_source": "proof_lane_decline_context_miner_project_source_candidate",
            "identifier_from_decline_note": "ssytFinset",
            "kind": "def",
            "lean_snippet": "noncomputable def ssytFinset (lam : NPartition N) : Finset (SSYT lam) :=\n  (ssytFillingFinsetNonSkew lam).attach.map ⟨\n    fun ⟨f, hf⟩ => fillingToSSYT lam f (Finset.mem_filter.mp hf).2,\n    fun ⟨f₁, hf₁⟩ ⟨f₂, hf₂⟩ h => by\n      simp only [Subtype.mk.injEq]\n      have : (fillingToSSYT lam f₁ (Finset.mem_filter.mp hf₁).2).entries =\n             (fillingToSSYT lam f₂ (Finset.mem_filter.mp hf₂).2).entries := by\n        simp only [fillingToSSYT] at h\n        cases h\n        rfl\n      exact this\n  ⟩\n\n/-- Every SSYT is in ssytFinset. -/",
            "line": 1211,
            "match_kind": "exact_local_name",
            "name": "SymmetricFunctions.ssytFinset",
            "path": "algebraic-combinatorics/AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.lean",
            "safety_policy": "Candidate came from a target-hidden proof-lane decline note and project-source scan. Same-source aligned/referencing target declarations were used only as an exclusion filter and are not included in this pack."
          },
          {
            "context_source": "proof_lane_decline_context_miner_project_source_candidate",
            "identifier_from_decline_note": "Nipat",
            "kind": "structure",
            "lean_snippet": "structure Nipat (lam mu : Fin N → ℕ)\n    (hlam : ∀ i j : Fin N, i ≤ j → lam j ≤ lam i)\n    (hmu : ∀ i j : Fin N, i ≤ j → mu j ≤ mu i)\n    (hcontained : ∀ i, mu i ≤ lam i) where\n  /-- The tuple of paths, one for each i ∈ [N] -/\n  paths : (i : Fin N) → LatticePath (N := N)\n    ((mu i : ℤ) - (i.val : ℤ)) ((lam i : ℤ) - (i.val : ℤ))\n  /-- The paths satisfy column-strictness: for i < j, if east steps k (in path i)\n      and k' (in path j) correspond to the same tableau column (mu i + k = mu j + k'),\n      then the height of path i at step k is strictly less than the height of path j at step k'.\n      This is the key property that makes the nipat-SSYT bijection work. -/\n  colStrictPaths : ∀ i j : Fin N, i < j →\n    ∀ k : ℕ, ∀ hk : k < (paths i).eastStepHeights.length,\n    ∀ k' : ℕ, ∀ hk' : k' < (paths j).eastStepHeights.length,\n    mu i + k = mu j + k' →\n    (paths i).eastStepHeights[k] < (paths j).eastStepHeights[k']\n\n/-- The weight of a nipat is the product of the weights of its component paths. -/",
            "line": 2872,
            "match_kind": "exact_local_name",
            "name": "SymmetricFunctions.Nipat",
            "path": "algebraic-combinatorics/AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.lean",
            "safety_policy": "Candidate came from a target-hidden proof-lane decline note and project-source scan. Same-source aligned/referencing target declarations were used only as an exclusion filter and are not included in this pack."
          },
          {
            "context_source": "proof_lane_decline_context_miner_project_source_candidate",
            "identifier_from_decline_note": "SkewSSYT",
            "kind": "structure",
            "lean_snippet": "structure SkewSSYT {N : ℕ} [NeZero N] (lam mu : NPartition N) extends SkewYoungTableau lam mu where\n  /-- Entries increase weakly along rows -/\n  row_weak : ∀ i : Fin N, ∀ j₁ j₂ : ℕ,\n    (i, j₁) ∈ skewYoungDiagram lam mu → (i, j₂) ∈ skewYoungDiagram lam mu → j₁ < j₂ →\n    entry (i, j₁) ≤ entry (i, j₂)\n  /-- Entries increase strictly down columns -/\n  col_strict : ∀ i₁ i₂ : Fin N, ∀ j : ℕ,\n    (i₁, j) ∈ skewYoungDiagram lam mu → (i₂, j) ∈ skewYoungDiagram lam mu → i₁ < i₂ →\n    entry (i₁, j) < entry (i₂, j)\n\n/-! ## Properties of Semistandard Skew Tableaux -/\n\n/-- In a semistandard skew tableau, entries increase weakly along rows (general version).\n    Lemma \\ref{lem.sf.skew-ssyt.increase}(a) in the source. -/",
            "line": 1960,
            "match_kind": "exact_full_name",
            "name": "SkewSSYT",
            "path": "algebraic-combinatorics/AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean",
            "safety_policy": "Candidate came from a target-hidden proof-lane decline note and project-source scan. Same-source aligned/referencing target declarations were used only as an exclusion filter and are not included in this pack."
          },
          {
            "context_source": "proof_lane_decline_context_miner_project_source_candidate",
            "identifier_from_decline_note": "SkewSSYT",
            "kind": "structure",
            "lean_snippet": "structure SkewSSYT (s : SkewPartition N) where\n  /-- The entries of the tableau, only for boxes in Y(λ/μ).\n      Entry (i, k) corresponds to box (i, μᵢ + k + 1) in Y(λ/μ). -/\n  entries : (i : Fin N) → (k : Fin (s.outer.parts i - s.inner.parts i)) → Fin N\n  /-- Entries are weakly increasing along rows -/\n  rowWeak : ∀ i : Fin N, ∀ j k : Fin (s.outer.parts i - s.inner.parts i),\n    j ≤ k → entries i j ≤ entries i k\n  /-- Entries are strictly increasing down columns.\n      If boxes (i, c) and (i', c) are both in Y(λ/μ) with i < i', then\n      T(i, c) < T(i', c). Here c = μᵢ + k + 1 for some k.\n      The condition checks: for row i and column offset k, if row i+1 also\n      contains column μᵢ + k + 1 (i.e., μᵢ + k + 1 > μᵢ₊₁ and μᵢ + k + 1 ≤ λᵢ₊₁),\n      then the entry in row i is strictly less than the entry in row i+1. -/\n  colStrict : ∀ i : Fin N, ∀ hi : i.val + 1 < N,\n    ∀ k : Fin (s.outer.parts i - s.inner.parts i),\n    -- The absolute column is μᵢ + k + 1. For this to be in row i+1:\n    -- 1. μᵢ + k + 1 > μᵢ₊₁ (column is after the inner partition in row i+1)\n    -- 2. μᵢ + k + 1 ≤ λᵢ₊₁ (column is within the outer partition in row i+1)\n    ∀ _hcol : s.inner.parts i + k.val + 1 > s.inner.parts ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ ∧\n             s.inner.parts i + k.val + 1 ≤ s.outer.parts ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩,\n    -- The offset in row i+1 for the same column\n    let k' : ℕ := s.inner.parts i + k.val - s.inner.parts ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩\n    ∀ hk' : k' < s.outer.parts ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ - s.inner.parts ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩,\n    entries i k < entries ⟨i.val + 1, hi⟩ ⟨k', hk'⟩\n\n/-- The monomial x^T associated to a tableau T.\n    x_T = ∏_{(i,j) ∈ Y(λ)} x_{T(i,j)} -/",
            "line": 926,
            "match_kind": "exact_local_name",
            "name": "SymmetricFunctions.SkewSSYT",
            "path": "algebraic-combinatorics/AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.lean",
            "safety_policy": "Candidate came from a target-hidden proof-lane decline note and project-source scan. Same-source aligned/referencing target declarations were used only as an exclusion filter and are not included in this pack."
          },
          {
            "context_source": "proof_lane_decline_context_miner_project_source_candidate",
            "identifier_from_decline_note": "potentialVerticalStrips",
            "kind": "def",
            "lean_snippet": "def potentialVerticalStrips (mu : NPartition N) : Finset (Fin N → ℕ) :=\n  Fintype.piFinset (fun i => Finset.Icc (mu.parts i) (verticalStripUpperBound mu i))\n\n/-- The set of N-partitions λ such that λ/μ is a vertical n-strip.\n\n    This is the set of all N-partitions λ satisfying:\n    1. μ ⊆ λ (containment): μ_i ≤ λ_i for all i\n    2. Vertical strip: λ_i ≤ μ_i + 1 for all i\n    3. Size: |λ| - |μ| = n\n\n    The set is finite because each λ_i ∈ {μ_i, μ_i + 1}. -/",
            "line": 1509,
            "match_kind": "exact_local_name",
            "name": "SymmetricFunctions.potentialVerticalStrips",
            "path": "algebraic-combinatorics/AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.lean",
            "safety_policy": "Candidate came from a target-hidden proof-lane decline note and project-source scan. Same-source aligned/referencing target declarations were used only as an exclusion filter and are not included in this pack."
          }
        ],

        "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.tex",
        "referenced_labels": [],
        "source_text": "\\begin{theorem}\n[Second Jacobi--Trudi formula]\\label{thm.sf.jt-e}Let $\\lambda$ and $\\mu$ be\ntwo partitions. Let $\\lambda^{t}$ and $\\mu^{t}$ be the transposes of $\\lambda$\nand $\\mu$. Let $M\\in\\mathbb{N}$ be such that both $\\lambda^{t}$ and $\\mu^{t}$\nhave length $\\leq M$. We extend the partitions $\\lambda^{t}$ and $\\mu^{t}$ to\n$M$-tuples (by inserting zeroes at the end). Write these $M$-tuples\n$\\lambda^{t}$ and $\\mu^{t}$ as $\\lambda^{t}=\\left(  \\lambda_{1}^{t}%\n,\\lambda_{2}^{t},\\ldots,\\lambda_{M}^{t}\\right)  $ and $\\mu=\\left(  \\mu_{1}%\n^{t},\\mu_{2}^{t},\\ldots,\\mu_{M}^{t}\\right)  $. Then,%\n\\[\ns_{\\lambda/\\mu}=\\det\\left(  \\left(  e_{\\lambda_{i}^{t}-\\mu_{j}^{t}%\n-i+j}\\right)  _{1\\leq i\\leq M,\\ 1\\leq j\\leq M}\\right)  .\n\\]\n\n\\end{theorem}"
      },

                    "kind": "theorem",
                    "lean_snippet": "/-- First Jacobi-Trudi formula (Theorem thm.sf.jt-h)\n    Label: thm.sf.jt-h\n\n    s_{λ/μ} = det((h_{λᵢ - μⱼ - i + j})_{1 ≤ i,j ≤ M})\n\n    The proof uses the Lindström-Gessel-Viennot lemma with appropriate\n    source and target vertices in the integer lattice ℤ².\n\n    ## Proof Strategy\n\n    **Step 1: Set up the LGV framework**\n    Define source vertices Aᵢ = (μᵢ - i, 1) and target vertices Bⱼ = (λⱼ - j, N) in ℤ².\n    The path weight matrix M_{i,j} = ∑_{p : Aᵢ → Bⱼ} w(p) where w(p) is the product\n    of x_h for each east-step at height h.\n\n    **Step 2: Identify M with the Jacobi-Trudi matrix**\n    By `pathWeightSum_eq_hsymm`, M_{i,j} = h_{λⱼ - μᵢ - j + i} = h_{λⱼ - μᵢ - (j - i)}.\n    Note: The textbook uses 1-indexed notation; our Lean formalization uses 0-indexed.\n    The matrix entry (i,j) is h_{λᵢ - μⱼ - i + j} in the textbook convention.\n\n    **Step 3: Apply the LGV lemma**\n    By `lgv_nonpermutable` (Corollary cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np), since the source and\n    target vertices satisfy the sorting conditions (x-decreasing, y-increasing),\n    we have:\n      det(M) = ∑_{nipats from 𝐀 to 𝐁} w(nipat)\n    where the sum is only over non-intersecting path tuples (nipats).\n\n    **Step 4: Establish the nipat-SSYT bijection**\n    By `nipat_ssyt_bijection`, there is a weight-preserving bijection between\n    nipats from 𝐀 to 𝐁 and SSYT of skew shape λ/μ:\n    - The nipat (p₁, ..., pₖ) maps to the tableau T where row i contains\n      the heights of east-steps in path pᵢ (in weakly increasing order).\n    - Non-intersection of paths ensures column-strictness of the tableau.\n    - The weight w(nipat) = ∏ᵢ w(pᵢ) equals the monomial x^T.\n\n    **Step 5: Conclude**\n    det(M) = ∑_{nipats} w(nipat) = ∑_{SSYT T of shape λ/μ} x^T = s_{λ/μ}\n\n    ## Key Lemmas Required",
                    "line_range": [
                      8001,
                      8066
                    ],
                    "module": "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.PieriJacobiTrudi",

                    ],

                    "lean_snippet": "theorem nipat_ssyt_bijection (lam mu : Fin N → ℕ)\n    (hlam : ∀ i j : Fin N, i ≤ j → lam j ≤ lam i)\n    (hmu : ∀ i j : Fin N, i ≤ j → mu j ≤ mu i)\n    (hcontained : ∀ i, mu i ≤ lam i) :\n    ∃ f : Nipat lam mu hlam hmu hcontained →\n        SkewSSYT ⟨⟨lam, hlam⟩, ⟨mu, hmu⟩, fun i => hcontained i⟩,\n      Function.Bijective f ∧\n      ∀ np, np.weight (R := R) = (f np).toMonomial",

              "referenced_labels": [],
              "source_text": "\\begin{theorem}\n[Pieri rules]\\label{thm.sf.pieri}Let $n\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Let $\\mu$ be an\n$N$-partition. Then: \\medskip\n\n\\textbf{(a)} We have%\n\\[\nh_{n}s_{\\mu}=\\sum_{\\substack{\\lambda\\text{ is an }N\\text{-partition;}%\n\\\\\\lambda/\\mu\\text{ is a horizontal }n\\text{-strip}}}s_{\\lambda}.\n\\]\n\n\n\\textbf{(b)} We have%\n\\[\ne_{n}s_{\\mu}=\\sum_{\\substack{\\lambda\\text{ is an }N\\text{-partition;}%\n\\\\\\lambda/\\mu\\text{ is a vertical }n\\text{-strip}}}s_{\\lambda}.\n\\]\n\n\\end{theorem}"
            }
          },
          {
            "reason": "Earlier or explicitly referenced source theorem-like unit.",
            "source_unit": {
              "environment": "theorem",
              "id": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.tex:thm.sf.jt-h",
              "labels": [
                "thm.sf.jt-h"
              ],
              "line_range": [
                192,
                208
              ],
              "parse_warnings": [],
              "part_markers": [],
              "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/SymmetricFunctions/PieriJacobiTrudi.tex",
              "referenced_labels": [
                "def.sf.skew-schur",
                "rmk.sf.N-par-vs-M-par"
              ],
              "source_text": "\\begin{theorem}\n[First Jacobi--Trudi formula]\\label{thm.sf.jt-h}Let $M\\in\\mathbb{N}$. Let\n$\\lambda=\\left(  \\lambda_{1},\\lambda_{2},\\ldots,\\lambda_{M}\\right)  $ and\n$\\mu=\\left(  \\mu_{1},\\mu_{2},\\ldots,\\mu_{M}\\right)  $ be two $M$-partitions\n(i.e., weakly decreasing $M$-tuples of nonnegative integers). Then,%\n\\[\ns_{\\lambda/\\mu}=\\det\\left(  \\left(  h_{\\lambda_{i}-\\mu_{j}-i+j}\\right)\n_{1\\leq i\\leq M,\\ 1\\leq j\\leq M}\\right)  .\n\\]\n(Here, $s_{\\lambda/\\mu}$ is defined as in Definition \\ref{def.sf.skew-schur},\nwhere the semistandard tableaux of shape $\\lambda/\\mu$ are defined as certain\nfillings of $Y\\left(  \\lambda/\\mu\\right)  :=\\left\\{  \\left(  i,j\\right)\n\\ \\mid\\ i\\in\\left[  M\\right]  \\text{ and }j\\in\\left[  \\lambda_{i}\\right]\n\\setminus\\left[  \\mu_{i}\\right]  \\right\\}  $, but their entries are still\nsupposed to be elements of $\\left[  N\\right]  $. Compare with Remark\n\\ref{rmk.sf.N-par-vs-M-par}.)\n\\end{theorem}"
            }
          }
        ],
        "source_focus_summary": "Second Jacobi-Trudi formula using elementary symmetric functions"
      }
    }
